questions.questionText: What is the first thing a technician should check when a client reports no internet?
options: Replace the router immediately
Check if the POE device and router are powered on
Reinstall the antenna
Format the client’s computer

questions.questionText: If the POE device has no lights on, what is the most likely issue?
options: Antenna misalignment
Loose cable or no power supply
Overheating of router
Client exceeded bandwidth

questions.questionText: A client reports intermittent connectivity. The first on-site test should be:
options: Check cable color coding
Replace the antenna immediately
Ping the default gateway from a laptop connected via LAN
Reboot the entire WISP network

questions.questionText: If the signal strength from the Access Point is low, what is the most common solution?
options: Add more routers inside the house
Change the Wi-Fi password
Realign the antenna to face the correct AP direction
Replace the LAN cable

questions.questionText: If a client’s router WAN settings were accidentally reset, what should the technician do?
options: Switch the router to bridge mode
Call the WISP headquarters
Reconfigure the PPPoE credentials manually
Disconnect and reconnect the LAN cable

questions.questionText: What can frequent maintenance calls for a specific site indicate?
options: The client uses too many devices
There is likely a recurring issue with cable quality, antenna placement, or power stability
They need a new Wi-Fi password
The router is fully updated

questions.questionText: If Wi-Fi works on phones but not on the client’s PC, the issue is likely:
options: The antenna is broken
The cable is too short
The PC’s network settings or hardware
The ISP is down

questions.questionText: A POE adapter feels very hot and the client is losing internet often. What should be done?
options: Hide the adapter behind the TV
Replace the adapter and check for power source issues
Switch the POE to LAN port
Ignore it unless it burns

questions.questionText: What is an important behavior a technician should maintain when dealing with a frustrated client?
options: Blame the client for the issue
Speak in technical jargon to sound smart
Stay calm, listen actively, and explain the situation clearly
Rush the job to finish quickly

questions.questionText: When should a technician escalate an issue to network-level support?
options: If multiple nearby clients are reporting the same outage
When one client has low Wi-Fi signal
After changing the Wi-Fi name
When the router LED is green
